title: ceratonia oreothauma subsp. somalensis
intro: Ceratonia oreothauma subsp. somalensis, also known as the Somali carob tree, is a subspecies of Ceratonia oreothauma, a plant that belongs to the legume family and is native to the Horn of Africa. The Somali carob tree is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. The plant's fruits, seeds, and leaves have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, and its health benefits include reducing the risk of heart disease and certain types of cancer. In herbal preparations, the Somali carob tree is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken orally to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
